svg.aria-label: Animated diagram: minnal WAL entries are appended, marked persisted on memtable flush, and whole segments are reclaimed by WAL garbage collection, keeping on-disk WAL size bounded.
svg.text: Every write is fsynced into the shared log; a memtable flush marks its entries Persisted; WAL GC then deletes the segments the log no longer needs.

g.text: wal.log
active
reclaimed by GC
wal.log.seg000001
active
reclaimed by GC
wal.log.seg000002
active
reclaimed by GC
wal.log.seg000003
active
reclaimed by GC
head
put() → WAL append + fsync. Entry 1 lands in segment 0 as Inserted.
put() → WAL append + fsync. Entry 2 lands in segment 0 as Inserted.
put() → WAL append + fsync. Entry 3 lands in segment 0 as Inserted.
Segment 0 is full (64 MB) → segment 1 opens as the active tail.
Writes continue in segment 1. Nothing is reclaimable yet — every entry is still Inserted.
Memtable flush → L0. Those keys are marked Persisted — segment 0 is fully covered.
WAL GC (60s): segment 0 is fully persisted and not the active tail → file deleted, head advances.
New writes append to the tail. On disk the WAL holds only what recovery still needs.
Segment 1 fills up → segment 2 opens as the new active tail.
Segment 1's entries are still Inserted — their memtable has not been flushed yet.
GC tick: segment 1 still has un-persisted entries, segment 2 is active → nothing to reclaim.
The next flush lands in L0 → segment 1's entries flip to Persisted.
WAL GC: segment 1 deleted, head advances. total_entries / persisted_entries drop with it.
The pattern repeats: segment 2 fills, segment 3 becomes the active tail.
Flush + GC reclaim segment 2. The active segment is never reclaimed — it is the live tail.
Steady state: the WAL stays bounded — un-persisted entries plus the active tail, nothing more.

svg.text: WAL ENTRIES ON DISK, OVER TIME
Illustrative: 4 entries per segment. Real segments are 64 MB.
